title: Down Easy Into Sleep
intention: To let go of the day and drift gently into sleep without ever needing to wake.

lines.text: Well now. There you are.
You've come a long way today, cher. You can set it all down now.
Let your head be heavy on the pillow. Let it sink right in.
No need to do a thing from here on. Just lay there and let me do the talking.
Take one slow breath in through the nose.
And let it go, easy, out through the mouth.
There. That's it.
Another one, nice and slow. In.
And out, like a long sigh at the end of a long day.
You don't have to make the breath do anything. Just let it come and go on its own.
I'm gonna slow right down now. And you can come along with me.
Feel the weight of your shoulders letting go of your ears.
Feel your back resting heavy on the bed beneath you.
The mattress holds you. You don't have to hold yourself up no more.
Let your jaw go soft. Let your tongue rest easy in your mouth.
Let that little space between your eyebrows smooth right out.
Your face is soft now. Like still water out on the bay before dawn.
Let your arms get heavy. Heavy as wet sand.
Your hands, your fingers. All of it letting go.
And down through your hips, your legs, your feet.
Heavy. Warm. Sinking down.
Picture an old wharf at the end of the evening.
The tide is high and slow. The boats just rocking, easy.
You can hear the water moving under the boards. Soft and steady.
There's a light way out on the water. Just a small one.
And it's already going dim. You don't need to keep your eye on it.
Let it blur. Let it slip out of focus.
The wharf is fading now too. The edges getting soft.
You can't quite tell where the water ends and the dark begins.
And that's just fine. Let it all come undone.
Breathe in, slow.
And out, slower still.
Each breath out, you sink a little deeper into the bed.
A little heavier. A little further away.
There's nowhere you've got to be. Nothing left to tend to tonight.
The day is behind you now, like a road in the dark you can barely make out.
And you don't have to look back at it.
Let your thoughts get slow and far apart.
Like fog coming in off the water. Soft. Quiet.
If a thought drifts by, just let it drift on past.
No need to follow it. No need to finish it.
I'm gonna talk less now. And the quiet's gonna get bigger.
Just rest. Just lay there and rest.
Heavy and warm. Held by the bed.
Every breath a little quieter than the one before.
The fog is all around you now. Soft and grey and kind.
You can let the pictures go. You don't need them no more.
Just the dark. Just the breath. Just resting.
Sinking down, slow, like a stone settling on the soft bottom.
Nothing to hold. Nothing to keep.
Let go a little more.
There you go, cher. Down easy.
Rest now.
All is well. Sleep.
